calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-27-2018 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-16-2017 Set For Trial On Dec-17-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion For Relief From Waiver Of Discovery Objections; Declaration Of Corban J. Porter In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Apr-02-2018 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order To Have Requests For Admission Deemed Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $1,490.00 Against Defendant
Off Calendar Mar-14-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Kate Hersh-Boyle, Esq. 415-441-5544 of Hersh & Hersh for Plaintiff LEWIS RAYNOR, HAVEN HERRIN.
Corban Porter, Esq. 925-222-3400 of WSH & B for Defendant Dr. Crane.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares his views. Plaintiff argues, reports responses recieved are not code compliant, repetitive. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF LEWIS RAYNOR, HAVEN HERRIN's Motion For An Order To Have Requests For Admission Deemed Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $1,490.00 Against Defendant, Defendant's Attorneys, or both.

Plaintiffs' motion for an order to have requests for admission deemed admitted and for monetary sanctions is denied in its entirety. While it is true that there is no pre-filing meet and confer requirement for this motion, the court concludes that, had plaintiffs' counsel contacted counsel for Dr. Crane in advance of the motion, Dr. Crane would have agreed to provide the requests for admission responses without need for filing the motion. Under these circumstances, it cannot be said that the motion needed to be filed and thus plaintiffs' request for monetary sanctions is denied. Dr. Crane's belated code-compliant responses to the requests for admission make it unnecessary to compel any further responses.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order To Have Requests For Admission Deemed Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $1,490.00 Against Defendant
Off Calendar Feb-21-2018 Continued To Mar-14-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Kate Hersh-Boyle, Esq. of Hersh & Hersh, T:415.441.5544, for plaintiffs/moving party;
Corban J. Porter, Esq. of Wood Smith Henning & Berman LLP, T:925.222.3419, for defendant/opposing party.

Both counsel did not stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court vacates the tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS LEWIS RAYNOR, HAVEN HERRIN's Motion For An Order To Have Requests For Admission Deemed Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $1,490.00 Against Defendant is continued to March 14, 2018 at 9:30 am in Department 302. The Judge Pro Tem shall prepare his Report and Recommendation.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Not reported.

Off Calendar Nov-16-2017 Off Calendar

Off Calendar Nov-08-2017 Reset From Nov-08-17 To Nov-15-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-25-2017 Set For Trial On Nov-05-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-18-2017 Continued To Nov-08-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-16-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Corban Porter, Esq. of Wood Smith Henning & Berman LLP, Tel. 925.222.3419, for defendant and moving party Curtis Crane, M.D.;
Nancy Hersh, Esq. of Hersh & Hersh, Tel. 415.444.5544, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT CURTIS CRANE DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINTas follows: Defendant Crane's demurrers to the first and second causes of action are overruled. Even if the court accepts Dr. Crane's premise that failure to obtain informed consent is a species of medical negligence, plaintiff's theories of recovery (negligent surgery and failure to obtain informed consent) are not duplicative. There is no practical purpose in requiring plaintiff to replead his failure to obtain informed consent claim under the rubric of medical negligence in the first cause of action. Dr. Crane is not prejudiced by any purported pleading defect. The demurrers to the intentional and negligent misrepresentation claims are overruled. The amended complaint pleads pre and post operative misrepresentations. While the bodies of the misrepresentation claims only refer to the pre-operative statements, those claims incorporate by reference all of the pre and post operation allegations. Dr. Crane is correct that generally pre-operative misstatements support only a lack of informed consent cause of action, but Stone v. Foster (1980) 106 Cal.App.3d 334, 347 does not entirely foreclose a fraud claim. The claims are alleged with sufficient particularity and less facts are necessary where the defendant has presumptive knowledge of the claim. Plaintiff correctly notes that he is allowed to plead inconsistent theories of recovery. (See Mendoza v. Continental Sales Co. (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 1395, 1402.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Gordon F. Aiavao, CSR # 11216, email: gaiavao@sftc.org.
